etymology.description: 醫(醫) (yī, "to heal") + 生(生) (shēng, "life; birth; grow; raw") = a person who heals life -> doctor
etymology.interpretation: 醫(醫) (yī, "to heal") combines the idea of treating and curing illnesses. 生(生) (shēng, "life; birth; grow; raw") refers to life and the act of giving life or allowing life to continue. Together, 醫生(醫生) (yīshēng, "doctor") conveys the role of a person who heals and restores life, emphasizing the doctor's function to maintain and improve health, thus 'doctor'.

examples.chinese: 我想成为一名医生(我想成為一名醫生)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng chéngwéi yī míng yīshēng.
examples.english: I want to become a doctor.

examples.chinese: 医生给我开了药(醫生給我開了藥)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng gěi wǒ kāi le yào.
examples.english: The doctor prescribed medicine for me.

examples.chinese: 这个医生很有经验(這個醫生很有經驗)。
